summary: Validated the pending-order abandon flow end-to-end. Backend: 6/6 pytest pass (200 deleted, 200 already_gone idempotency, 409 paid, 403 cross-user, 401 unauth, 200 unknown-id idempotent). Frontend wiring: all 7 booking pages (Cinema, Travel, Hotel, CarRental, Restaurant, Event, Package) import useOrderAbandonment and pass onCheckoutAbandoned to PaymentMethodsSelection — verified by grep (3 hook touchpoints per file). PaymentMethodsSelection invokes onCheckoutAbandoned from BOTH the Stripe modal onClose (line 732-733) AND the MoMo closeMoMoDialog handler (line 256-257). Live UI-generated pending cinema order (f6fcd6a3...) was successfully hard-deleted via the abandon endpoint and confirmed 404 on subsequent GET — proving the endpoint works on real orders. ONE BUG FOUND AND FIXED: useOrderAbandonment.js beforeunload handler read localStorage.getItem('token') but the app stores the JWT under 'access_token' — would have caused 401 on tab-close path. Patched to 'access_token'.

integration_issues.flow: Tab close / browser refresh during open payment modal
integration_issues.issue: useOrderAbandonment beforeunload handler read wrong localStorage key ('token' instead of 'access_token') — would send unauthenticated DELETE → 401 → orphan not deleted on tab close. FIXED in this iteration.

action_items: (Optional) Add an e2e Playwright assertion that bypasses the country-selector modal first (dismiss it via Skip for Now before navigating to /services/cinema/...) so future runs can drive Confirm → Close Stripe modal → DB 404 without getting stuck on the location dialog.

(Nice-to-have) Add a data-testid='stripe-modal-close' on the Stripe payment modal X button so e2e tests can target it deterministically instead of relying on aria-label='Close' which collides with other dialogs (e.g. country-selector).
critical_code_review_comments: Backend DELETE /api/orders/{id}/abandon is well-scoped — idempotent, 403 cross-user, 409 paid — exactly what was specified. Good.

useOrderAbandonment beforeunload uses fetch with keepalive (correct for unload paths) but originally referenced the wrong token key. After this iteration's fix, both unmount (axios + access_token) and beforeunload (fetch + access_token) paths agree.

Banquet and Laundry bookings intentionally pass orderId={null} per the request — confirmed by main agent. Worth a one-line code comment in those files so a future contributor doesn't think the wiring is missing.

success_rate.frontend: Wiring 100% (all 7 booking pages + PaymentMethodsSelection verified by grep); live API verification on a real UI-generated cinema order PASSED; full Playwright click-Confirm → close-Stripe-modal → assert-404 path was blocked by an unrelated country-selector modal — see action items

seed_data_creation: Created 4 TEST_ cinema orders via /api/orders/create during pytest; the happy-path order was hard-deleted by the test, the 409 paid-order test left one paid TEST_ row behind (by design — paid orders can't be abandoned), the 403 cross-user test cleaned up after itself, idempotent test cleaned up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The country-selector ('Select Your Location') modal can appear on first visit to /services/cinema/film/* for users without a saved location and BLOCKS the booking flow. Future e2e tests should pre-set the location in localStorage (key: 'selected_country' or similar — check LocationContext) or click [data-testid='location-skip'] before navigating. Token key is 'access_token' app-wide; only useOrderAbandonment had the wrong key, now fixed. To verify the abandon hook live: log in as customer → /services/cinema/film/1edf5c04-f19a-4808-9b3c-b8e168e8dc02 → showtime 58702e65 → seat → Confirm → close Stripe modal → check that the pending order disappears via GET /api/orders/{id} → 404.
rca of the issue: Token-key mismatch in /app/frontend/src/hooks/useOrderAbandonment.js line 37 — the beforeunload handler read 'localStorage.getItem("token")' while the rest of the app (AuthContext.jsx line 128, api/client.js line 45, PaymentMethodsSelection.jsx, etc.) consistently uses 'access_token'. Result: the keepalive fetch fired on tab-close would have no Authorization header → 401 → pending order would survive in db.orders, defeating the entire purpose of this fix for the tab-close case. Fix: one-line change to use 'access_token'. Unmount path (uses the axios client which already reads 'access_token') was already correct, so router-navigation cleanup worked; only tab-close / refresh was broken.
